# Billplz Payment API

**OpenAPI spec included** · [View spec →](./3.0.0/openapi.json) · *Generated by Jentic — the vendor does not publish an official OpenAPI specification.*

Generate payment bills, manage collections, process FPX bank transfers, and handle tokenized card payments for Malaysian businesses through 37 endpoints.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Base URL** | `https://www.billplz.com/api` |
| **Auth** | Basic Auth |
| **Endpoints** | 37 |

## What you can build with this API

- Issue bills with unique payment links and track individual transaction status in real time
- Organize bills into collections and open collections with custom branding and split-payment rules
- Accept FPX direct-debit bank transfers by listing supported banks and routing payments
- Tokenize customer card details for recurring charges without storing raw card data
- Receive webhook callbacks on payment completion to trigger fulfillment workflows

Does not handle accounting, invoicing with line items, or multi-currency payments — use it for Malaysian ringgit bill collection and payment acceptance.

## Key endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | /v3/bills | Create a bill |
| GET | /v3/bills/{bill_id} | Get bill details |
| GET | /v3/bills/{bill_id}/transactions | List transactions for a bill |
| POST | /v3/collections | Create a collection |
| POST | /v3/open_collections | Create an open collection |
| GET | /v4/banks | List FPX banks |

## Key resources

| Resource | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Bills | Create, retrieve, and delete payment bills with unique payment URLs |
| Collections | Group bills under branded collection pages with split rules |
| Transactions | View payment transaction history per bill |
| FPX Banks | List supported Malaysian banks for direct-debit transfers |
| Tokenization | Store card details as tokens for repeat charges |
| Payment Orders | Manage batch payment disbursements |

### Python quickstart

Jentic sits between your agent and this API. The agent searches by intent, loads operation details, and executes — while credentials stay encrypted in the vault, isolated from the agent.

```python
import asyncio
from jentic import Jentic, SearchRequest, LoadRequest, ExecutionRequest

async def main():
    client = Jentic()
    search = await client.search(SearchRequest(query="create a payment bill and get a payment link"))
    entity_id = search.results[0].id
    details = await client.load(LoadRequest(ids=[entity_id]))
    result = await client.execute(
        ExecutionRequest(
            id=entity_id,
            inputs={"collection_id": "abc123", "email": "customer@example.com", "name": "Ahmad Ali", "amount": 5000, "description": "Monthly subscription"}
        )
    )
    print(result)

asyncio.run(main())
```

Works with LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Agent SDK, and any MCP-compatible framework.
